symptoms.symptom_name: Thick white clumpy 'cottage cheese' vaginal discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A thick, white, curd-like discharge is characteristic of candidal vulvovaginitis.
Bacterial vaginosis produces a thin, grey, homogeneous discharge — a clumpy curd-like discharge argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense vulvar pruritus with external raw burning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense vulvar itch is one of the most discriminating symptoms of candidiasis versus bacterial vaginosis.
Irritant vulvitis also itches and burns, but without a curd-like discharge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent completed course of broad-spectrum antibiotics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recent broad-spectrum antibiotics suppress protective lactobacilli and are a well-recognised precipitant of candidal overgrowth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a foul or fishy odour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fishy odour (positive whiff test) is typical of bacterial vaginosis; its absence reduces that probability.
Trichomoniasis often has a malodorous discharge; absence of odour makes it less likely though does not exclude it.
Vulvar inflammation causes stinging as urine contacts irritated skin and pain with intercourse — consistent with candidal vulvitis rather than an internal cystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well, no pelvic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A localised, afebrile presentation without pelvic pain is consistent with simple vulvovaginitis rather than ascending pelvic infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vulvovaginal erythema and oedema on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vulvar and vaginal erythema/oedema with adherent curd-like discharge supports candidiasis — but this is a clinician examination finding that cannot be obtained via chat; the AI Doctor infers from the patient's own description, not a measured exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wet mount microscopy showing budding yeast and pseudohyphae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Budding yeast and pseudohyphae on wet mount confirm candidiasis, but this test is not performed in this encounter and is not required to treat an uncomplicated episode.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a curd-like discharge, intense vulvar itch, a recent antibiotic course and the absence of a fishy odour is a characteristic, high-probability picture of uncomplicated candidal vulvovaginitis that justifies empirical antifungal treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: High vaginal swab / self-collected vaginal swab (only if atypical, recurrent, or non-responsive)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a characteristic uncomplicated first-line episode. Reserve for treatment failure, recurrent disease, diagnostic uncertainty, or pregnancy. Can be arranged through the GP rather than urgently.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: STI testing (if risk factors or discharge features suggest an STI)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Consider if the odour, frothy discharge, or sexual-risk history raise trichomoniasis or another STI — arranged at GP follow-up, not urgently in this presentation.
There is no indication for urgent investigations in an afebrile, systemically well, localised vulvovaginitis — ordering them would be over-investigation.
